summary: Frontend testing on https://habio-web.preview.emergentagent.com is fully BLOCKED by CORS. The live production backend at https://api.habioapp.co returns HTTP 400 'Disallowed CORS origin' on the preflight OPTIONS /api/auth/login when called from the preview origin. As a result login fails in the browser and none of the 13 requested features (sidebar nav, /activity, reactions, /profile, /profile/edit, /people search, /u/orbitfriend follow, followers/following, /feed, /notifications, /weekly-recap, /habits/choose) could be exercised end-to-end through the UI. A direct curl POST to /api/auth/login (no Origin enforcement) returns 200 with a valid JWT for habiotest+web1@example.com / TestPass123!, confirming the backend itself is healthy and the credentials are correct — the failure is purely a backend CORS allowlist gap, not a frontend bug.

critical.issue: CORS preflight rejected with 400 'Disallowed CORS origin' when Origin is https://habio-web.preview.emergentagent.com. Response headers include 'access-control-allow-methods', 'access-control-allow-credentials: true', 'access-control-allow-headers: content-type' but NO 'access-control-allow-origin', so the browser blocks every XHR. Body literally returns 'Disallowed CORS origin'. This blocks 100% of authenticated UI flows from the preview URL.

integration_issues.issue: Browser console: 'Access to XMLHttpRequest at https://api.habioapp.co/api/auth/login from origin https://habio-web.preview.emergentagent.com has been blocked by CORS policy: Response to preflight request doesn't pass access control check: No Access-Control-Allow-Origin header is present.' Login submit stalls and user never reaches /dashboard; protected routes redirect back to /login.

action_items: Add https://habio-web.preview.emergentagent.com (and probably the wildcard *.preview.emergentagent.com) to the CORS allowlist on the production backend at api.habioapp.co. Verify by running:  curl -i -X OPTIONS https://api.habioapp.co/api/auth/login -H 'Origin: https://habio-web.preview.emergentagent.com' -H 'Access-Control-Request-Method: POST' -H 'Access-Control-Request-Headers: content-type'  — expect 200/204 with access-control-allow-origin echoing the origin.

Alternative (frontend-only) workaround if the backend allowlist cannot be changed quickly: add a CRA dev/proxy setup OR a reverse-proxy at the preview ingress so the SPA calls a same-origin path (e.g., /api/*) which is then proxied to api.habioapp.co; this avoids CORS entirely and matches the typical Emergent preview pattern (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL pointing to the preview host).
After fix, re-run frontend tests — once login works, every other testid in the request is wired up correctly in code (verified by static review), so the rest should pass with minimal churn.
critical_code_review_comments: Layout.jsx (NAV_ITEMS): 'Friend Feed', 'My Activity', 'Find People', 'Notifications', 'Weekly Recap', 'Profile' are all present and routed; notification unread badge uses data-testid='nav-unread-badge' and polls every 30s — looks good.
ActivityTimeline.jsx uses optimistic reaction updates with rollback on error — solid pattern; testids react-like-<id> / react-cheer-<id> match the spec.

Profile/EditProfile/UserSearch/PublicProfile/Followers/Following/Notifications/WeeklyRecap/ChooseHabit pages all expose the requested data-testids (verified by grep). No code-level bug found that would block the listed flows once CORS is resolved.
Minor: AuthContext stores token under 'habio_token' in localStorage and validates via /auth/me on mount — depends on the same CORS endpoint, so it will also fail today.
Frontend .env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is correctly set to https://api.habioapp.co with no trailing slash.

success_rate.frontend: 0% (blocked at login by CORS; static code review of all listed pages/testids passes)

test_credentials: habiotest+web1@example.com / TestPass123! (webtester) and habiotest+friend1@example.com / TestPass123! (orbitfriend) — both verified valid via direct curl POST to /api/auth/login (HTTP 200, JWT returned).
seed_data_creation: None. No follows, habits, or profile edits were persisted because all UI actions failed before reaching the backend.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run the same Playwright flow as soon as the CORS allowlist on api.habioapp.co includes the preview origin. The script in this iteration covered: login -> sidebar nav check -> /activity timeline + like/cheer toggle -> /profile + stats + Edit Profile bio + public toggle save -> /people search 'orbit' -> /u/orbitfriend follow toggle x2 -> view-activity nav -> followers/following pages -> /feed -> /notifications mark-all-read -> /weekly-recap -> /habits + habit-templates-button -> /habits/choose category chips + multi-select + create-habits-button. All selectors are correct per code; only CORS blocks them. Use cleanup step at the end to unfollow orbitfriend so live data stays minimal.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: Production backend (FastAPI behind nginx at api.habioapp.co) has a strict CORS origin allowlist that does NOT include the Emergent preview host https://habio-web.preview.emergentagent.com. REPRODUCTION: 1) Open https://habio-web.preview.emergentagent.com/login in a browser, 2) submit valid credentials, 3) DevTools Network shows OPTIONS /api/auth/login -> 400 with body 'Disallowed CORS origin' and no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header, 4) the subsequent POST is never sent and the UI hangs on the Log in button. CONFIRMATION: curl -i -X OPTIONS https://api.habioapp.co/api/auth/login -H 'Origin: https://habio-web.preview.emergentagent.com' -H 'Access-Control-Request-Method: POST' -H 'Access-Control-Request-Headers: content-type' returns HTTP/2 400 + 'Disallowed CORS origin'. The same backend returns 200 to a non-browser POST (no Origin policing) proving the data path is fine. MITIGATION: Update the backend CORSMiddleware allow_origins (or allow_origin_regex) to include the preview host, OR add a same-origin reverse proxy on the preview ingress so the SPA hits /api/* on its own origin and the proxy forwards to api.habioapp.co (this also avoids leaking the production origin into browser CORS at all).
